user_system_path: Between rungs. The live product thread is the GUARDIAN (workday-presence): it fires, grounds a real commitment, and pings Slack only when it has a reviewable move. This session shipped #402 (review-gated one-tap send from the Slack guardian ping, PR #403) and #404 (email draft attachments — the send carries the finished work, PR #405): the guardian now drafts → reviews → sends from the owner's own Gmail, and the email can carry the budget doc / forecast / memo as an attachment. The one live-readiness blocker is owner-side: set ALLOW_APPROVAL_EMAIL_SEND=true and validate a real attached send, plus the free external 15-min cron for guardian firing cadence. Remaining code candidate (not started): teach the generator to actually emit attachments (needs a prompt change + paid validation). Constraint: NO paid API calls to test — prove in the harness.
allowed_files_raw: Between rungs — only control-plane + docs may change until the owner names the next seam (which re-scopes this contract).
forbidden_files_raw: No product/runtime/schema/deps changes without an active seam + scoped contract.
acceptance_condition: Control plane is consistent in the between-rungs form (build-order active_issue: none, contract active_issue: none, no active-seam line in ACTIVE_HANDOFF) and gate:continuity passes.
stop_condition: Stop when the control plane is rolled forward to a clean between-rungs state and the owner has a boot prompt for the next session.
next_command: New session: read ACTIVE_HANDOFF.md + FOLDERA_MASTER_BIBLE.md Part II-B; the owner names the next seam — likely owner-side live validation of the review-gated attached send (flip ALLOW_APPROVAL_EMAIL_SEND) and the external 15-min guardian cron, or the code seam to teach the generator to emit attachments.
